titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Haldane Macfall Collection
titlestmt.author: Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11860356.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 11, 2018 at 20:17

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection includes manuscripts, typescripts, and proofs of books, essays, and plays including a biography of Aubrey Beardsley, and the novels The Splendid Wayfaring and The Three Students; originals and proofs of illustrations, bookplates, and cover art; correspondence; and contracts with publishers. The majority of the correspondence consists of Macfall's outgoing letters to Earl E. Fisk, a British-born book collector, anthologist and insurance agent who lived in Green Bay, Wisconsin, and assisted Macfall in placing his books with American publishers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The majority of the collection was purchased on the Francis O. Matthiessen Fund, 1956. Other items were the gifts of Mrs. Leon Godchaux, 1948, and E. L. Sampter, 1956.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Haldane Macfall Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing, in 2014.
The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as MS Vault Macfall.

The finding aid for this collection is largely compiled from individual catalog cards that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. Information from the catalog cards was repurposed in the finding aid in accordance with current archival descriptive standards. Some catalog cards, copies of which are filed with the collection, include more detailed descriptions of individual items and/or provenance notes. Descriptions for items not represented by catalog cards were derived from accession records, folder labels, or other available descriptive material.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Earl Fisk Papers (GEN MSS 342). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Haldane Macfall (1860-1928)
bioghist.p: Chambers Haldane Cooke Macfall was a British military officer who authored works on art history and criticism, several novels, and numerous essays. He designed and illustrated many of his own works as well as bookplates and cover art, and exhibited works at the Royal Academy. He was married to Mabel Plumridge Macfall. He died in 1928.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes manuscripts, typescripts and proofs of books, essays, and plays including Aubrey Beardsley , The Splendid Wayfaring , and The Three Students ; original and proofs of illustrations, bookplates, and cover art; correspondence; and contracts with publishers. The majority of the correspondence consists of Macfall's outgoing letters to Earl E. Fisk, a British-born book collector, anthologist and insurance agent who lived in Green Bay, Wisconsin, and assisted Macfall in placing his books with American publishers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two corrected typescripts, one with proofs of the illustrations inserted; carbon typescript of a critique of the book with autograph manuscript notations; and press clippings relating to the publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Page proofs, corrected.

title.part: The Book of Lovat Claud Fraser

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph manuscript drafts, one with proofs of illustrations inserted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript of the foreword accompanied by corrected page proofs of the book.

title.part: Ibsen: The Man, His Art, and His Significance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript, carbon, with manuscript corrections.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript of the title page and foreword, accompanied by page proofs of the book, volume 8 in his multi-volume work, A History of Painting .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript carbon with manuscript corrections.

did.unittitle: Songs of the Immortals [volume of poems edited by Haldane Macfall]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paste-up dummy, corrected, and illustrations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript "scheme" of the book prepared for Earl E. Fisk; carbon typescript preface, with manuscript corrections; typescript, carbon preface by Frank Brangwyn with manuscript copy in the hand of Earl E. Fisk; "A Personal Note on Haldane MacFall," by Edward Gordon Craig, typescript, corrected, accompanied by two carbon typescripts and galley proofs; typescript of the book with manuscripts corrections; paste-up dummy of proofs with illustrations; typescript, carbon, with manuscript corrections of the preface; and a copy of the book interleaved with clippings and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript, carbon, with manuscript corrections, bound with a photograph of his drawing for the endpaper for the first American edition (1926); and two typescripts with manuscript corrections.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts and proofs of a preface, corrected, by Earl E. Fisk; two trial book covers; and a scrapbook of clippings, reviews and correspondence concerning the book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript with manuscript corrections; accompanied by press clippings on Mussolini and the Scopes trial.

did.unittitle: "The Deaf Prophet of Prussia," autograph manuscript
"Pictures for Pence," typescript, with manuscript corrections
"Purification of the Unlicked Cub," two typescripts, corrected, with two fragmentary typescripts
"The Sentimental Ostler," typescript with manuscript corrections
"The Theatre and the Puritan," autograph manuscript address to the Players Club with bound manuscript, "The Puritan and the Theatre"
"The Third Man," by "Hal Dane," typescript, corrected
"Notes for an Address Upon the Ultimate Significance of Art," autograph manuscipt
Miscellaneous essay and prose writings, autograph manuscripts
Volumes containing clippings of essays by Haldane Macfall
"The Fear of Robert Clive," two typescripts, corrected
"The Three Students," typescript, corrected; with bound typescript carbon, corrected with typescript and manuscript notes laid in
Manuscript and typescript notes and writings by Haldane Macfall and Earl E. Fisk concerning a Macfall bibliography

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volumes containing press clippings, notes, and other ephemera relating to Macfall's writings.

did.unittitle: Pursuasions to Joy by Earl E. Fisk

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paste-up dummy of proofs with drawings by Haldane Macfall.

did.unittitle: Proofs of designs and cover title pages of books published by J.C. E.C. Jack
Trial bindings and cover designs for The Wooing of Jezebel Pettyfer and Poet's Country
Cover design for The Wooing of Jezebel Pettyfer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bookplate designs for Haldane Macfall, Earl A. Fisk, Alfred A. Knopf, and George R. B. Bellew.

did.unittitle: "Bargaining for Dinner in West Africa," pen and ink and water color drawing
"Enter the Queen," proof of illustration for The Three Students
"My Negro Magician," pen and ink drawing
Two small pen and ink drawings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters by Macfall to Earl E. Fisk, publishers, and admirers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from Macfall and Dion Clayton Cathrop's Rouge (London: Brown, Langham, and Co. Ltd.) 1906.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to Macfall by publishers and admirers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes contracts with Harper, Simon & Schuster, and Knopf.
